# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE   # you can add this directly in the pipeline file for your demo environment, move to GitLab CI/CD variables after testing. Replace it with actual values as per requirements or encrypted credentials storage (like HashiCorp Vault).
  AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY # you can add this directly in the pipeline file for your demo environment, move to GitLab CI/CD variables after testing. Replace it with actual values as per requirements or encrypted credentials storage (like HashiCorp Vault).
  
deploy_prod: 
  stage: deploy    
  before_script:    # This is the script that will be executed first for jobs inside this pipeline definition/stage and each job in parallel execution of these stages. You can include scripts to clean up after yourself (remove files, stop processes if necessary). If you want no such a thing just remove it!      
  image: docker  
  services:    # This is where we'll define the service that our jobs will use from your Dockerfile or other containers. You can have multiple in parallel execution of these stages for different AWS instances (DynamoDB, RDS).     //You should not replace this block when you are testing locally with docker-commands only
    - name: s3/boto3  # Name is used to identify the service within Docker. In our case we'll use boto for Amazon S3 and a generic one like 's3'. This way, AWS credentials can be passed when calling this specific Python module (e.g., in `__init__(self):` or as environment variables).
      url: s3           # URL is the service name where Docker will pull from your registry of images for execution inside a container created by us with services declared above. In our case it would be 's3'. This can vary based on what you've built in `setup_environment` function, so that we ensure consistent setup within each step/job or section being run
      host: $AWS_S3_HOST # If there are multiple services to this named service above and they need same AWS credentials set via environment variables then use "${ENV}" variable in the script as below. (example - url : s3, Aws::REGION = $awsRegion)
  script:          # This is where you'll write all your jobs/tasks inside Dockerfile that will be executed by this pipeline definition when it gets triggered via GitLab CI runner API from .gitlab-ci.yml file in YAML syntax, as defined above for "deploy_prod"
    - echo 'Deployment script goes here' # replace with your actual deployments tasks such as uploading code to s3 or running serverless function etc...  like so: aws s3 sync /path/to/my-project ./s3://bucketname   and export AWS_S3_HOST="$awsRegion.s3.amazonaws.com".
